pending.reason: Digitale-Versorgung-Gesetz (2019). Introduced DiGA, enabled digital health applications on prescription. Referenced in DiGA and AMTS articles. Key enabling legislation for TI digitalization wave.
Federal supervisory authority for qualified trust service providers (VDA) that issue QES certificates. Referenced in QES and PKI articles as regulator of the eIDAS trust chain for HBA certificates.
Digitale Pflegeanwendungen. Sister concept to DiGA for nursing care context, reimbursed by Pflegeversicherung, regulated by BfArM. Mentioned in DiGA and BfArM articles. Growing in importance.
Apothekenverwaltungssystem. The primary pharmacy software, mentioned in TI-Gateway, Konnektoren, AMTS, Fachdienst. Parallel to PVS for pharmacies. Central in E-Rezept dispensing workflow.
Elektronischer Medikationsplan. Central instrument of digital AMTS. Described in detail in AMTS article as integrated into ePA since March 2026. Supersedes the paper-based BMP. Deserves its own page.

Elektronisches Gesundheitsberuferegister. Register for health professions not covered by traditional chambers (nurses, midwives, physiotherapists). Mentioned in HBA as the HBA issuer for these groups. Increasingly relevant.
Infektionsschutzgesetz. Legal basis for DEMIS and infection reporting obligations. Mentioned in DEMIS article (3x). Foundational law for public health surveillance digitalization.

Unique identifier for all TI participants (practitioners, institutions). Referenced in VZD (LDAP entries), IDP (token claims), HBA/SMC-B certificates. Central concept for identity in the TI, mentioned across multiple articles without its own page.

Trust Service Provider. Certificate issuers in the TI-PKI, accredited by gematik. Described in PKI article but deserves own page: role in TI trust chain, accreditation process, relationship to eIDAS qualified VDAs.
Internationale Klassifikation der Krankheiten, German Modification. Maintained by BfArM, used in ISiK profiles (ISiKDiagnose), DEMIS meldungen. Fundamental coding standard in German healthcare IT.
Bundesministerium für Gesundheit. Holds 51% of gematik and sets TI policy direction. Referenced in gematik, BfArM, BSI articles. Key political actor in TI governance.
Krankenversicherungsnummer. Unique patient identifier used in TI-Federation tokens, Gesundheits-ID, VSDM. Referenced in TI-Federation and Gesundheits-ID articles as a key claim in OAuth tokens.
